other_observers: Mark Lockwood; Mark Lockwood found the bird on this date.
description: The bird was typical for a Plegadis ibis, being about 2 feet tall, having relatively long legs, glossy brown-green-maroon overall plumage, and long decurved bill. The bill was somewhat yellowish-gray in shade. Compared to nearby White-faced Ibis, this bird had blue feathers along the edge of the facial skin that extended from the base of the maxilla and mandible posterior to about the lower and upper center of the eye that formed pale blue lines emanating from the bill. Instead of being red, the facial skin (lores) was a very dark gray. The eyes were dark brown, not red. The mantle and wings seemed a bit more bluish than adjacent White-faced Ibis, which seemed to have a bit more yellowish-green cast to those areas. The legs were mainly a dull brownish-dark gray color, although the tibia-tarsus joints were maroon in color and contrasted with the majority of the leg.Behaviors: The Glossy, as well as the White-faced, were feeding in a wet, grassy field. They would drop and lift their heads continually in a sewing machine manner as they walked through the tall grass.Call: The ibis flock (mostly White-faced) were giving a grunting call as they fed.Plumage: alternate
